seo_title: Google's Agentic AI for Science: Frameworks and Applications
meta_description: This paper details two novel frameworks for developing autonomous, agentic AI in scientific workflows using Google Colab and large language model cloud…
body_html:
This paper details two novel frameworks for developing autonomous, agentic AI in scientific workflows using Google Colab and large language model cloud backends.

Both systems leverage a hybrid Local Body, Remote Brain architecture via Google Colab, utilizing Python-based local orchestrators to invoke large language model (LLM) cloud backends. The first agent, DeepTS/DeepCollector, automates the large-scale curation, extraction, and deduplication of time-series datasets. The second, DeepScribe, is an autonomous presentation analyzer that converts visually dense, mathematically complex physics lectures into structured scientific reports.

Key Features of DeepTS/DeepCollector

Granular Attribute Extraction (Cellular RAG) A practical systems engineering approach to automate the extraction of relevant attributes from time-series datasets.

Remote Data Inspection A method for inspecting and validating remote data in real-time, ensuring data quality and integrity.

Distributed Concurrency Controls A framework for managing concurrent access to distributed systems, ensuring efficient and secure data processing.

Autonomous Presentation Analyzer Converts visually dense, mathematically complex physics lectures into structured scientific reports. Utilizes large language model cloud backend for accurate and efficient analysis.

Generalization of DeepTS to Support Deep Knowledge Graphs This conceptual approach can be generalized to support deep knowledge graphs, enabling more comprehensive and nuanced scientific workflows.

source.title: Experiments in Agentic AI for Science
source.summary: Researchers have developed two novel frameworks for autonomous, agentic AI in scientific workflows using Google Colab and Python-based local orchestrators. The first framework, DeepTS/DeepCollector, automates data curation and extraction from time-series datasets, while the second, DeepScribe, converts complex physics lectures into structured reports. These systems leverage hybrid Local Body, Remote Brain architecture to overcome limitations of current state-of-the-art AI in scientific workflows.
